description_long: This awning consists of two adjacent units, each with a distinct canopy structure. The left unit features a solid, opaque white canopy supported by a robust frame, while the right unit displays an open, slatted wooden pergola design. Both units are mounted against a rectangular building facade, with the canopies projecting outward to provide shade and shelter. The support structures are composed of visible wooden beams and posts, giving the awning a modern yet natural aesthetic. The canopies extend over a paved area, likely intended for pedestrian or seating use, and the overall construction appears sturdy and architecturally integrated. No visible branding or decorative trim is present, emphasizing a clean and functional design.
description: A dual-section awning with one solid white canopy and one open wooden pergola, both supported by wooden beams and mounted to a building facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the solid white canopy on the left and the open wooden pergola on the right, highlighting the contrast in canopy styles and the supporting beams.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the canopies and the arrangement of the wooden slats on the pergola section.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain building facade and the attachment points of the awning structures, with minimal visible detail on the canopies.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the solid canopy's overhang and the open structure of the pergola, with clear visibility of the mounting surfaces.
attribute_canopy_pattern: solid color on left, open slatted on right
attribute_edge_trim_style: straight edge with no decorative trim
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with wooden beams and posts
